name: Evolution
description: Analog variable-character ladder LPF. Re-engineered E-mu 1100 core adds voltage-controlled pole count (3–6), Q-level compensation and Species distortion-drive. DC-coupled · >10-octave range · precise 1V/oct self-oscillation.

controls.name: FREQUENCY
controls.description: Initial cutoff frequency. Summed with all frequency CVs.

controls.name: FREQ CV3 attenuator
controls.description: Conventional attenuator for FREQ CV3 input.
controls.detail: CCW: no CV · CW: full amplitude at 1V/oct

controls.name: FREQ CV2 attenuverter
controls.detail: CCW: full inverted · noon: 0 · CW: full direct · both ends at 1V/oct

controls.name: Q
controls.detail: 0 – 7V effective range · approximately linear VCA-controlled Q

controls.name: Q LEVEL COMPENSATION
controls.description: Restores passband level lost as Q rises. CCW: classic ladder thinning. CW: full pre-attenuation body preserved.
controls.detail: Continuous mix between thin/resonant-only and full-body signal

controls.name: GENUS
controls.description: Initial number of active poles in the ladder. Continuous analog current-steering, not switched.
controls.detail: 3 poles (18dB/oct) – 6 poles (36dB/oct) · 5V swing covers full range

controls.name: SPECIES
controls.description: Signal level into ladder. Controls distortion of frequencies near/above cutoff.
controls.detail: 0 – 10V range · up to noon: subtle · past noon: audible grit

inputs.name: 1V/OCT
inputs.description: Calibrated full-level pitch CV for cutoff · 10+ octave tracking

behaviors.name: Voltage-controlled pole count
behaviors.description: Steers current through individual ladder stages in an analog continuous manner between 3 and 6 poles. LEDs show current integer pole count; two adjacent LEDs indicate the control is mid-transition. Modulating at LFO rates reshapes the filter's slope dynamically; at audio rates produces entirely new timbres not available from classic ladder filters.

behaviors.name: Genus shifts resonant pitch
behaviors.trigger: GENUS change with Q near or at self-oscillation
behaviors.description: The 180° phase-shift frequency differs per pole count: at 3 poles resonance sits 10 semitones above cutoff, 4 poles equal to cutoff, 5 poles 6 semitones below, 6 poles 10.5 semitones below. Modulating Genus during self-oscillation sweeps the pitch between these anchor points — arpeggiator-like patterns from a single CV.

behaviors.name: Oscillation continuity vs break
behaviors.trigger: Q at maximum vs just at onset · moving Genus
behaviors.description: With Q maxed, oscillation is continuous through all Genus transitions. With Q set just at self-oscillation threshold, moving Genus causes Evolution to stop oscillating between pole settings (the Q needed for oscillation differs per pole). Pick threshold-Q for gated arpeggios, max-Q for smooth pitch sweeps.

behaviors.name: Q Level Compensation
behaviors.description: Classic ladders attenuate the passband as Q rises, giving the familiar thin resonant wah. CCW preserves that behavior. CW progressively mixes the pre-ladder signal back in, keeping body and low-end intact under high resonance — useful for resonant pads and fat bass that would otherwise sound gutted.

behaviors.name: Species drive vs oscillation
behaviors.description: Because Q feedback lives inside the drive VCAs, Species does not affect resonance height but does inversely control self-oscillation amplitude. When self-oscillating, higher Species = quieter oscillator; Species CV thus amplitude-modulates the oscillator output. Combining audio-rate Species CV with an input signal produces ring-mod-like cross-modulation.

behaviors.name: Sine-wave VCO mode
behaviors.description: Self-oscillation produces a clean high-fidelity sine. 1V/OCT tracks accurately over 10+ octaves (better than many Eurorack VCOs). Purest sine with Genus at 4 poles and Q just barely into oscillation. Evolution doubles as a sine-only VCO with filter FM/AM capabilities via Species.

behaviors.name: DC-coupled CV processing
behaviors.description: DC-coupled throughout. Patching an LFO or envelope into INPUT lets Evolution smooth, slew or amplify CVs. With Genus at 3 poles the response is a slow RC-style lag; at 6 poles a steeper response. Self-oscillating with CV in causes quadrature-like behavior useful for complex modulation shapes.

behaviors.name: Three frequency CVs sum
behaviors.trigger: 1V/OCT + FREQ CV2 + FREQ CV3 all patched
behaviors.description: 1V/OCT is full-level fixed. FREQ CV2 has an attenuverter (sign-flippable). FREQ CV3 has a plain attenuator (positive only). All three sum with the FREQUENCY knob. Gives simultaneous key-tracking, bidirectional envelope and scaled LFO modulation without external mixers.

patchIdeas.name: Slope-morphing bass
patchIdeas.patch: Genus modulated by slow LFO ±2.5V · Q ~noon (no oscillation) · bass oscillator into INPUT · cutoff at 150Hz · filter slope morphs between 18 and 36 dB/oct cyclically, breathing the low-end character without changing pitch or resonance
patchIdeas.diagram:
Evolution:
* Frequency: ~150Hz
* Q: ~noon
* Genus: noon
* Genus CV2 attenuverter: ~3 o'clock

LFO:

VCO:

- VCO (Out) -> Evolution (INPUT)
- LFO (Out) >> Evolution (GENUS CV2)
- Evolution (OUTPUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Pole-count arpeggio
patchIdeas.patch: no input · Q maxed (continuous oscillation) · Frequency set to A4 · square LFO into GENUS CV1 · Evolution jumps between four oscillation pitches (+10, 0, -6, -10.5 semitones of cutoff) as Genus steps across pole counts — self-generating arpeggio
patchIdeas.diagram:
Evolution:
* Q: maximum
* Frequency: A4
* Genus: sweep range

LFO:

- LFO (Out) >> Evolution (GENUS CV1)
- Evolution (OUTPUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Precision sine VCO
patchIdeas.patch: no input · Q just at oscillation · Genus at 4 poles · sequencer 1V/oct into 1V/OCT · Evolution becomes a clean, 10-octave-tracking sine VCO — use as a fifth voice or sub when your VCOs are maxed out
patchIdeas.diagram:
Evolution:
* Q: at oscillation threshold
* Genus: 4 poles
* Frequency: base pitch

Sequencer:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> Evolution (1V/OCT)
- Evolution (OUTPUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Through-zero filter FM
patchIdeas.patch: Genus 5 poles · Q high · audio-rate VCO into FREQ CV2 with attenuverter swept through noon · bipolar FM crosses zero · harsh metallic inharmonic tones built from ladder-filter self-oscillation
patchIdeas.diagram:
Evolution:
* Genus: 5 poles
* Q: high (near oscillation)

Modulator VCO:

Audio VCO:

- Audio VCO (Out) -> Evolution (INPUT)
- Modulator VCO (Out) >> Evolution (FREQ CV2)
- Evolution (OUTPUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Fat resonant bass
patchIdeas.patch: Q ~3 o'clock · Q LEVEL COMPENSATION fully CW · cutoff 200Hz tracked with keyboard · Species at ~1 o'clock for warmth · the low body stays full under high resonance instead of thinning — aggressive acid bass without the classic ladder thin-out
patchIdeas.diagram:
Evolution:
* Q: ~3 o'clock
* Q Level Compensation: full CW
* Species: ~1 o'clock
* Frequency: ~200Hz

Bass VCO:

Keyboard:

- Bass VCO (Out) -> Evolution (INPUT)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Evolution (1V/OCT)
- Evolution (OUTPUT) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: Species ring-mod
patchIdeas.patch: self-oscillating · audio-rate sine into SPECIES CV1 · Species now amplitude-modulates the oscillator output (inverse to Q feedback) · combine with a carrier signal into INPUT for ring-mod-like sum-and-difference tones
patchIdeas.diagram:
Evolution:
* Q: maximum
* Species: noon
* Genus: 4 poles

Modulator:

Carrier:

- Carrier (Out) -> Evolution (INPUT)
- Modulator (Out) >> Evolution (SPECIES CV1)
- Evolution (OUTPUT) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Generative slew
patchIdeas.patch: CV source into INPUT (DC-coupled) · Q low · Genus 3 poles · Frequency ~100Hz · Evolution acts as a gentle slew limiter on the CV · modulate Genus from another LFO to dynamically shift smoothing character from soft (3 poles) to sharp (6 poles)
patchIdeas.diagram:
Evolution:
* Genus: 3 poles (start)
* Q: low
* Frequency: ~100Hz

S&H:

Shape LFO:

VCO:

- S&H (Out) >> Evolution (INPUT) // CV processed
- Shape LFO (Out) >> Evolution (GENUS CV1)
- Evolution (OUTPUT) p> VCO (V/Oct) // dynamic glide

patchIdeas.name: Dual CV envelope LPF
patchIdeas.patch: keyboard 1V/oct into 1V/OCT · envelope into FREQ CV2 with attenuverter noon→CW for positive sweep · LFO into FREQ CV3 with attenuator low for vibrato · three-source CV sum via the three independent frequency inputs with no external mixer
patchIdeas.diagram:
Evolution:
* Q: ~2 o'clock
* Frequency: ~500Hz

Envelope:

LFO:

Keyboard:

Source:

- Source (Out) -> Evolution (INPUT)
- Keyboard (Pitch) p> Evolution (1V/OCT)
- Envelope (Out) >> Evolution (FREQ CV2)
- LFO (Out) >> Evolution (FREQ CV3)
- Evolution (OUTPUT) -> VCA (In)
